memoryArtifacts.headline: Trim-Castle- in County Meath Ireland was built by Hugh de Lacy and his son Walter de Lacy
memoryArtifacts.description:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trim_Castle
Trim Castle (Irish: Caisleán Bhaile Atha Troim) is a Norman castle on the south bank of the River Boyne in Trim, County Meath, Ireland. With an area of 30,000 m², it is the largest Norman castle in Ireland. Over a period of 30 years, it was built by Hugh de Lacy and his son Walter as the caput of the Lordship of Meath.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Rohese de Clare於1090年出生在英國英格蘭肯特托布利其托布利其 卡素時，她的父親Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford25歲，母親Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont26歲。她於1130年與Baderon of Monmouth在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾坡斯托福結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1175年在英國英格蘭享年85歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 度布林度布林。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rohese de Clare于1090年出生在英国英格兰肯特托布利其托布利其 卡素时，她的父亲Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford25岁，母亲Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont26岁。她于1130年与Baderon of Monmouth在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾坡斯托福结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1175年在英国英格兰享年85岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 度布林度布林。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Rohese
meanings: An old British name. It is found Latinised Rhesus, and is probably derived from the Greek word for Mars, the god of war.

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When Rohese de Clare was born in 1090, in Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, was 25 and her mother, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, was 26. She married Baderon of Monmouth in 1130, in Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1175, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85, and was buried in Dublin, County Dublin, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rohese de Clare est née en 1090, à Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Baderon of Monmouth en 1130, à Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1175, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterrée à Dublin, County Dublin, Irlande.

Sie hat 1130 in Chepstow, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom Baderon of Monmouth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1175 im Alter von 85 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Dublin, County Dublin, Irland begraben.

ko.Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지 토브리지 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Rohese de Clare(이)가 1090년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지 토브리지 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1130년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 체프스타우에서 Baderon of Monmouth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1175년 영국 잉글랜드에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 드블린 드블린에 묻혔다.

ru.Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Engla: тонбридж кастл, тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rohese de Clare родилась в 1090 г. в местности тонбридж кастл, тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baderon of Monmouth в 1130 г. в местности чепстоу, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1175 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 85 лет и была похоронена в местности дублинь, коунти дублинь, Ирландия.
